failed_rules.message: LoanType must be Closed End Second for 2nd Lien program
US Citizens are not eligible for Select ITIN program.
FICO Score score 715 below minimum requirement 720
FICO Score score 715 below minimum 720 required for Primary Residence, SFR
Income Doc Type "1099: 12 Mo." is not a DSCR type - Investor DSCR requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Occupancy 'Primary Residence' not eligible for Foreign National. Available: Investment Property, Second Home
US Citizens are not eligible for Foreign National program - FAIL
Income Doc Type "1099: 12 Mo." is not a DSCR type - DSCR Supreme Program requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Income Doc Type "1099: 12 Mo." is not a DSCR type - DSCR Multi (5-8 Units) requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL
Income Doc Type "1099: 12 Mo." is not a DSCR type - Investor DSCR No Ratio requires a DSCR income documentation type - FAIL

document_requests.description: Tri-merge credit report or Residential Mortgage Credit Report (RMCR) for all borrowers
specifications: Must be a tri-merge report from all three major repositories (Experian, TransUnion, Equifax)
Must include credit scores from all reporting bureaus
Must show complete tradeline history with payment patterns
Must display all public records including judgments, liens, and bankruptcies
Must show all credit inquiries within the past 24 months
Must identify any disputed accounts or fraud alerts
Must show collections, charge-offs, and derogatory items
Must verify Social Security Number
Must certify public record searches for all residences in the last 2 years
Must be dated within program-allowed recency window
Must include housing/mortgage payment history if any mortgage tradelines exist
reasons_needed: Credit score is required to validate Flex Supreme program eligibility and pricing
Tradeline requirements must be verified: 3 tradelines reporting 12+ months OR 2 tradelines reporting 24+ months with activity in last 12 months
Credit report is required to identify any credit events, bankruptcies, foreclosures, or housing events
Must verify no derogatory mortgage history or housing events that would affect eligibility
Required to review inquiries and identify any potential undisclosed debts
Public record searches required to identify liens, judgments, or other encumbrances
NQMF guidelines mandate tri-merge or RMCR for all borrowers on the loan

specifications: Must be completed on FNMA Form 1004 (Uniform Residential Appraisal Report) for single-family residence
Must identify subject property address matching the purchase contract and loan application
Must confirm property type as single-family residence (SFR) and unit count as 1
Must show appraised value supporting the $411,000 purchase price and 80% LTV calculation
Must be dated within 120 days of the Note date; if older than 120 days, Form 1004D recertification required
Must include all pages and addenda
Must include street map showing location of subject and all comparables
Must include exterior building sketch with dimensions and floor plan with GLA calculations
Must include original color photographs of front, street, and rear views of subject property
Must include interior photos of kitchen, all bathrooms, main living area, and any physical deterioration or renovations
Must include comparable sales analysis and appraiser certification
Must confirm property condition is average or better (C1-C4; C5/C6 not acceptable)
Must confirm no health or safety issues, structural deficiencies, or excessive deferred maintenance
Must confirm property meets minimum 400 sq ft requirement and contains full kitchen and bathroom
Must confirm property usage represents highest and best use
Must confirm property is accessible and available for year-round residential use
Must identify and comment on any adverse environmental or economic conditions
Must include analysis of any sales/listings of subject property within prior 3 years
Must include appraiser's license/certification in the state of California
Must include copy of appraiser's E&O Insurance Policy or AMC confirmation on file
reasons_needed: Collateral valuation required for underwriting per NQMF guidelines
LTV/CLTV eligibility depends on appraised value supporting $328,800 loan at 80% LTV
Property type and unit count must be verified for program eligibility
Property condition must meet NQMF minimum property standards (no fair/poor ratings)
Subject property identity must be consistent across all loan file documents
NQMF requires full interior inspection with specific photo requirements
Appraisal Independence Policy compliance required under Dodd-Frank

specifications: Must be unexpired government-issued photo identification
Must display full legal name matching loan application
Acceptable forms: State driver's license, state ID card, US passport, or permanent resident card
Must be legible with clear photo visible

reasons_needed.reason: Borrower identity verification required for all loan transactions per compliance requirements
Name on ID must match loan application and all supporting documents

specifications: Must be fully executed with borrower signature and date
Must include correct borrower SSN and current address
Must authorize IRS transcript retrieval for most recent 2 tax years
Must list correct property address as applicable
Form must be dated within 120 days of closing

reasons_needed.reason: IRS Form 4506-C required per NQMF Full Documentation guidelines for all income sources
Tax transcript authorization enables verification of reported income against IRS records

document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Full Documentation - IRS Form 4506-C requirement

specifications: Must certify borrower's intended occupancy of subject property as primary residence
Must include borrower signature and date
Must acknowledge understanding of occupancy fraud penalties
Must be consistent with loan application declared occupancy type

reasons_needed.reason: Primary residence occupancy must be certified for LTV, pricing, and program eligibility determination
Occupancy certification required to confirm consistency with Flex Supreme program requirements

document_requests.description: 12-month rental payment history verification for current housing
specifications: Must cover minimum 12 months of rental payment history
Must identify borrower as tenant on the rental agreement or lease
Must show monthly payment amounts and due dates
Must document any late payments (30+ days) during the period
Must be signed by landlord/property manager with contact information
Must include property address of rental unit
Acceptable alternative: 12 months cancelled checks or bank statements showing rental payments to same payee
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require verification of borrower's housing history for all programs
Housing history is required to assess borrower payment behavior for eligibility
12 month 0x30 housing/rental history may be required depending on program tier
Housing payment history not reporting on credit report requires separate VOR documentation
Acceptable housing history can serve as tradeline equivalent per guideline allowance
document_requests.condition: Required if borrower is currently renting and housing history does not appear on credit report

specifications: Must include all 1099 forms received for the most recent 12-month period
Must show borrower name matching loan application
Must show payer name and EIN
Must show gross income amounts
May include 1099-MISC, 1099-NEC, or 1099-K as applicable to income source
Must cover period consistent with bank statement income period if used together
reasons_needed: 1099 income program requires 1099 forms to document and verify independent contractor or self-employment income
Payer information required to verify legitimacy of income source
Income amounts on 1099s must reconcile with bank deposits and tax return reporting
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Full Documentation - 1099 Income Program (12 months)

specifications: Must include complete personal tax return (Form 1040) for most recent tax year
Must include all schedules (Schedule C, Schedule E, Schedule SE as applicable)
Must be signed and dated or include IRS transcript
Must show borrower name and SSN consistent with loan application
Must include all pages and attachments
Must support 1099 income declared on loan application
Must be included with Form 1040 for most recent tax year
Must show business name and principal business activity
Must show gross receipts and net profit/loss
Must show borrower as proprietor
Must support self-employment income calculation
reasons_needed: Tax returns required to verify 1099 income reported to IRS
Schedule C or other schedules needed to calculate net self-employment income
Income must be verified for stability and continuity per NQMF guidelines
Tax return income must reconcile with 1099 forms and bank statement deposits
Schedule C documents sole proprietor income from 1099 sources
Net profit calculation required for qualifying income determination
Business expenses and depreciation must be reviewed for add-backs per NQMF cash flow analysis
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Full Documentation - Self-Employment Income requirements

specifications: Must cover most recent 2 consecutive months
Must include all pages of each statement
Must show borrower name as account holder
Must show financial institution name and logo
Must show account number (full or masked)
Must show beginning and ending balances for each month
Must show complete transaction history
Must be dated within 90 days of note date per NQMF guidelines
Must cover all accounts being used for funds to close and reserves
Any large deposits (typically exceeding 50% of monthly qualifying income) must be identified for sourcing
Must show verified liquid assets sufficient to cover required reserve months
Must identify acceptable asset sources per program guidelines
Must show account ownership by borrower
Must support required months of PITIA (Principal, Interest, Taxes, Insurance, Association dues)
Must exclude gift funds from reserve calculation per NQMF guidelines
Must be dated within 90 days of note date
Reserve calculation must account for all financed properties if applicable
reasons_needed: Assets must be verified for funds to close per NQMF guidelines requiring evidence of sufficient funds from acceptable sources for down payment, closing costs, prepaid items, and debt payoff
Reserves must be verified for DSCR/Non-QM purchase transactions
Account ownership must be confirmed to ensure borrower has legal access to funds
Transaction history required to identify large deposits that may need sourcing documentation
Balance verification supports funds to close calculation and reserve determination
Program requires reserves based on occupancy, property type, and loan characteristics
Reserve calculation must be supported by verified liquid assets
Non-QM/DSCR programs typically require 6-12 months reserves
Reserves provide risk mitigation for lender
document_requests.source_trigger: Always required for funds to close verification
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Assets Section - Assets must be dated within 90 days of note date

specifications: Must show EMD amount matching purchase contract
Must show date of deposit
Must show source account from which EMD was paid
Must include copy of cancelled check or wire confirmation
Must show escrow agent or title company receipt if deposited with third party
Must trace from borrower's verified bank account showing withdrawal
Must reconcile to purchase contract terms
reasons_needed: Purchase transaction requires verification of earnest money deposit
EMD funds must be traced to acceptable source (borrower's verified account)
EMD amount credited toward funds to close must be documented
Ensures EMD was not sourced from unacceptable funds
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Assets Section - Funds must be from acceptable sources for down payment

specifications: Must reflect FNMA SSR Score of 2.5 or less per NQMF appraisal review process
Must correspond to the appraisal submitted for the subject property
Must identify subject property address matching appraisal and loan file
If SSR score exceeds 2.5, alternative review product required (CDA, AVM, or Field Review)
reasons_needed: NQMF appraisal review process requires secondary appraisal product to support value
SSR score validates appraisal quality and value reasonableness
Standard collateral review requirement for 1-4 unit residential properties

specifications: Must be a Flood Certificate provided by FEMA-authorized vendor
Must identify subject property address matching loan file
Must clearly indicate flood zone designation in Section B4
Must indicate whether property is in Special Flood Hazard Area (SFHA) - Zone A or Zone V
Must align with flood zone indicated in appraisal report
Must include determination date and certification
reasons_needed: NQMF requires flood zone determination for all properties
Determines whether flood insurance is required (mandatory for Zone A or V)
Appraisal flood zone designation must be verified against official FEMA determination
Properties in SFHA must be in FEMA-participating community to be eligible

specifications: Must provide hazard insurance protecting against fire and standard extended coverage perils
Extended coverage must include wind, civil commotion, smoke, hail, aircraft, vehicle, and explosion
Must provide coverage on replacement cost basis (actual cash value not acceptable)
Must name all persons holding title to subject property as named insured
Must include standard mortgagee clause (loss payable clause not acceptable)
Must be effective for at least 60 days after date of funding
Must include policy number, loan number, coverage amounts, deductible, and premium
Must include policy effective and expiration dates
Must include insurance company name, agent information, and agent signature/date
Property address must match note/security instrument
Must require insurer notification to mortgagee before cancellation
reasons_needed: NQMF requires hazard insurance on all properties to protect collateral
Replacement cost coverage required per guidelines (ACV not acceptable)
Mortgagee clause protects lender's interest in collateral
Coverage must be in place at closing and maintained throughout loan term

specifications: Must identify borrower/owner/vested parties matching Veronica Marie Montes
Must identify subject property address and legal description for the California SFR
Must disclose all liens, judgments, easements, exceptions, and title requirements
Must show title insurer name and effective date
Must be dated within acceptable recency window (current)
Must include 24-month chain of title history per NQMF requirements
Must show clear first lien position for the new mortgage
Title insurer must be qualified and licensed in California
reasons_needed: Title must confirm ownership and insurable interest before loan approval
Vesting on title must align with borrower name on loan application
Liens and exceptions must be reviewed for impact on first lien position
24-month title history required per NQMF guidelines to identify any flipping activity or transfer inconsistencies
Title exceptions must be reviewed to determine if they are allowable per guidelines

specifications: Must show current ownership/vesting of the property
Must identify property address and legal description matching subject property
Must show grantee name(s) that will match final title policy vesting
For purchase transaction: may be the deed conveying property to seller (to confirm seller has authority to convey)
Recorded deed preferred; if pending, preliminary deed to be recorded at closing acceptable
reasons_needed: Confirms seller has clear title and authority to convey property in purchase transaction
Vesting must reconcile with borrower and transaction structure per NQMF guidelines
Final title policy vesting must reflect borrower name per NQMF requirements
Legal description must match between deed, title commitment, and appraisal

specifications: Must be prepared and signed by CPA, EA, or licensed tax preparer with PTIN
Must be dated within 60 days of application
Must show business name consistent with tax returns
Must show gross revenue, expenses by category, and net profit
Must cover year-to-date period from January 1 to current date
Preparer must attest they have prepared borrower's most recent tax returns
reasons_needed: P&L statement required to determine income stability and trending
YTD income must be compared to prior year to identify declining income patterns
NQMF guidelines require P&L to support self-employment income qualification
document_requests.guideline_reference: NQMF Self-Employment Income - P&L used to determine stability of income

specifications: Required only if income shows decline from prior year to current year
Must be signed and dated by borrower
Must explain reason for income decline
Must address whether decline is temporary or expected to continue
reasons_needed: NQMF guidelines require signed written explanation for declining income
Declining income sources must be closely reviewed to determine usability for qualification
If income is declining, the lower income amount must be used rather than averaging
